hypothesis: Si–M alloys (M = Cu, Ni, or Co) in which M forms a percolating intermetallic scaffold occupying ≥20 at.% of lattice sites will reduce volumetric expansion during full lithiation to ≤30% and preserve Li+ migration channel radius within ±0.2 Å across charge/discharge cycles, resulting in >80% capacity retention after 500 cycles in standard Li-ion coin cells.
